title: Calculating the equation of the least-squares line
description: Practice using summary statistics and formulas to calculate the equation of the least-squares line.
topic_path: Calculating the equation of the least-squares line

assessment_items.question_content:
A high school counselor wants to look at the relationship between the grade point average (GPA) and the number of absences for students in the senior class this past year. The data show a linear pattern with the summary statistics shown below:

 | mean | standard deviation
- | - | -
$x=\#\text{ of absences}$  | $\bar{x}=5.0$ | $s_x=1.2$
$y=\text{GPA}$ | $\bar{y}=2.9$ | $s_y=0.3$
 |  | $r=-0.65$

**Find the equation of the least-squares regression line for predicting GPA from the number of absences.**  
*Round your entries to the nearest hundredth.*

$\hat y=$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$+$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$x$
hints:
##Least-squares regression equation

The equation for the least-squares regression line for predicting $y$ from $x$ is of the form

$\hat{y}=a+bx$,

where $a$ is the $y$-intercept and $b$ is the slope.

##Finding the slope

We can determine the slope as follows:

$b=r\left(\dfrac{s_y}{s_x}\right)$

In our example,

$b=-0.65\left(\dfrac{0.3}{1.2}\right)=-0.1625$

##Finding the $y$-intercept

Because the regression line passes through the point $(\bar x, \bar y)$, we can find the $y$-intercept as follows:

$a=\bar y-b\bar x$

In our example,

$a=2.9+0.1625(5)=3.7125$

##Answer

$\hat y=3.71-0.16x$

assessment_items.question_content:
A personal trainer wants to look at the relationship between number of hours of exercise per week and resting heart rate of her clients. The data show a linear pattern with the summary statistics shown below:

 | mean | standard deviation
- | - | -
$x=$ hours of exercise per week  | $\bar{x}=8.9$ | $s_x=4.8$
$y=$ resting heart rate (beats per minute) | $\bar{y}=74.3$ | $s_y=7.2$
 |  | $r=-0.88$

**Find the equation of the least-squares regression line for predicting resting heart rate from the hours of exercise per week.**  
*Round your entries to the nearest hundredth.*

$\hat y=$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$+$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$x$
hints:
##Least-squares regression equation

The equation for the least-squares regression line for predicting $y$ from $x$ is of the form:

$\hat{y}=a+bx$,

where $a$ is the $y$-intercept and $b$ is the slope.

##Finding the slope

We can determine the slope as follows:

$b=r\left(\dfrac{s_y}{s_x}\right)$

In our example,

$b=-0.88\left(\dfrac{7.2}{4.8}\right)=-1.32$

##Finding the $y$-intercept

Because the regression line passes through the point $(\bar x, \bar y)$, we can find the $y$-intercept as follows:

$a=\bar y-b\bar x$

In our case,

$a=74.3 +1.32 (8.9)=86.048$

##Answer

$\hat y=86.05 -1.32 x$

assessment_items.question_content:
A fuel consortium chairman at an airport wants to look at the relationship between cost of crude oil and the cost of jet fuel. The data show a linear pattern with the summary statistics shown below:

 | mean | standard deviation
- | - | -
$x=$ cost per barrel of crude oil (dollars)  | $\bar{x}=57.4$ | $s_x=30.8$
$y=$ cost per barrel of jet fuel (dollars)| $\bar{y}=69.9$ | $s_y=35$
 |  | $r=0.99$

[http://marketrealist.com/2014/09/why-political-and-legal-factors-impact-airline-industry/]: (Source)

**Find the equation of the least-squares regression line for predicting the cost of jet fuel from the cost of crude oil.**  
*Round your entries to the nearest hundredth.*

$\hat y=$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$+$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$x$
hints:
##Least-squares regression equation

The equation for the least-squares regression line for predicting $y$ from $x$ is of the form:

$\hat{y}=a+bx$,

where $a$ is the $y$-intercept and $b$ is the slope.

##Finding the slope

We can determine the slope as follows:

$b=r\left(\dfrac{s_y}{s_x}\right)$

In our case,

$b=0.99\left(\dfrac{35}{30.8}\right)=1.125$

##Finding the $y$-intercept

Because the regression line passes through the point $(\bar x, \bar y)$, we can find the $y$-intercept as follows:

$a=\bar y-b\bar x$

In our case,

$a=69.9 -1.125 (57.4)=5.325$

##Answer

$\hat y=5.33 +1.13 x$

assessment_items.question_content:
An environmental activist wants to look at the relationship between number of incentive actions taken by the government and the percent share that electric cars make up of overall new vehicle sale in several metropolitan areas. The data show a linear pattern with the summary statistics shown below:

 | mean | standard deviation
- | - | -
$x=$ number of incentive actions  | $\bar{x}=12.2$ | $s_x=5.2$
$y=$ electric vehicle share of new vehicle sales (percent) | $\bar{y}=1.1$ | $s_y=1.3$
 |  | $r=0.65$

[http://theicct.org/sites/default/files/publications/ICCT_EV-promotion-US-cities_20150729.pdf]: (Source)

**Find the equation of the least-squares regression line for predicting the electrical vehicle share of new vehicle sales from the number of incentive actions the government takes.**  
*Round your entries to the nearest hundredth.*

$\hat y=$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$+$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$x$
hints:
##Least-squares regression equation

The equation for the least-squares regression line for predicting $y$ from $x$ is of the form:

$\hat{y}=a+bx$,

where $a$ is the $y$-intercept and $b$ is the slope.

##Finding the slope

We can determine the slope as follows:

$b=r\left(\dfrac{s_y}{s_x}\right)$

In our case,

$b=0.65\left(\dfrac{1.3}{5.2}\right)=0.1625$

##Finding the $y$-intercept

Because the regression line passes through the point $(\bar x, \bar y)$, we can find the $y$-intercept as follows:

$a=\bar y-b\bar x$

In our case,

$a=1.1 -0.1625 (12.2)=-0.8825$

##Answer

$\hat y=-0.88 +0.16 x$

assessment_items.question_content:
An ornithologist wants to look at the relationship between the breadth of peregrine falcon eggs and the mass of the falcon chicks that hatch from them. The data show a linear pattern with the summary statistics shown below:

 | mean | standard deviation
- | - | -
$x=$ egg breadth $(\text{mm})$  | $\bar{x}=41$ | $s_x=2.3$
$y=$ chick's mass $(\text{g})$ | $\bar{y}=33.6$ | $s_y=4.6$
 |  | $r=0.95$

[http://scholarworks.boisestate.edu/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?article=1177&context=bio_facpubs]: (Source)

**Find the equation of the least-squares regression line for predicting the chick's mass from the breadth of the egg.**  
*Round your entries to the nearest hundredth.*

$\hat y=$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$+$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$x$
hints:
##Least-squares regression equation

The equation for the least-squares regression line for predicting $y$ from $x$ is of the form:

$\hat{y}=a+bx$,

where $a$ is the $y$-intercept and $b$ is the slope.

##Finding the slope

We can determine the slope as follows:

$b=r\left(\dfrac{s_y}{s_x}\right)$

In our case,

$b=0.95\left(\dfrac{4.6}{2.3}\right)=1.9$

##Finding the $y$-intercept

Because the regression line passes through the point $(\bar x, \bar y)$, we can find the $y$-intercept as follows:

$a=\bar y-b\bar x$

In our case,

$a=33.6 -1.9 (41)=-44.3$

##Answer

$\hat y=-44.3 +1.9 x$

assessment_items.question_content:
Dmitri wants to look at the relationship between room temperature and the etching rate each time he has etched a circuit board with cupric chloride. The data show a linear pattern with the summary statistics shown below:

 | mean | standard deviation
- | - | -
$x=$ room temperature (degrees Celsius)  | $\bar{x}=20.9$ | $s_x=3$
$y=$ etching rate (micrometers per minute) | $\bar{y}=6.6$ | $s_y=0.7$
 |  | $r=0.99$

[http://techref.massmind.org/Techref/pcb/etch/cucl2.htm]: (Source)

**Find the equation of the least-squares regression line for predicting the etching rate from the room temperature.**  
*Round your entries to the nearest hundredth.*

$\hat y=$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$+$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$x$
hints:
##Least-squares regression equation

The equation for the least-squares regression line for predicting $y$ from $x$ is of the form:

$\hat{y}=a+bx$,

where $a$ is the $y$-intercept and $b$ is the slope.

##Finding the slope

We can determine the slope as follows:

$b=r\left(\dfrac{s_y}{s_x}\right)$

In our case,

$b=0.99\left(\dfrac{0.7}{3}\right)=0.231$

##Finding the $y$-intercept

Because the regression line passes through the point $(\bar x, \bar y)$, we can find the $y$-intercept as follows:

$a=\bar y-b\bar x$

In our case,

$a=6.6 -0.231 (20.9)=1.7721$

##Answer

$\hat y=1.77 +0.23 x$

assessment_items.question_content:
Blanca creates decorative wall hangings. She wants to look at the relationship between the diagonal length of the hangings and their weight. The data show a linear pattern with the summary statistics shown below:

 | mean | standard deviation
- | - | -
$x=$ diagonal length (inches)  | $\bar{x}=24.1$ | $s_x=12$
$y=$ weight (pounds) | $\bar{y}=12.9$ | $s_y=16.2$
 |  | $r=0.9$

**Find the equation of the least-squares regression line for predicting the weight of the wall hanging from its diagonal length.**  
*Round your entries to the nearest hundredth.*

$\hat y=$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$+$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$x$
hints:
##Least-squares regression equation

The equation for the least-squares regression line for predicting $y$ from $x$ is of the form:

$\hat{y}=a+bx$,

where $a$ is the $y$-intercept and $b$ is the slope.

##Finding the slope

We can determine the slope as follows:

$b=r\left(\dfrac{s_y}{s_x}\right)$

In our case,

$b=0.9\left(\dfrac{16.2}{12}\right)=1.215$

##Finding the $y$-intercept

Because the regression line passes through the point $(\bar x, \bar y)$, we can find the $y$-intercept as follows:

$a=\bar y-b\bar x$

In our case,

$a=12.9 -1.215 (24.1)=-16.3815$

assessment_items.question_content:
The choir sells tickets to its performances for an assortment of prices and gives some tickets away. The choir director wants to look at the relationship between the number of seats occupied at each performance and the ticket revenue for that performance. The data show a linear pattern with the summary statistics shown below:

 | mean | standard deviation
- | - | -
$x=$ number of seats occupied  | $\bar{x}=75.8$ | $s_x=14.8$
$y=$ ticket revenue (dollars) | $\bar{y}=696$ | $s_y=177.6$
 |  | $r=0.81$

**Find the equation of the least-squares regression line for predicting the ticket revenue from the number of seats occupied.**  
*Round your entries to the nearest hundredth.*

$\hat y=$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$+$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$x$
hints:
##Least-squares regression equation

The equation for the least-squares regression line for predicting $y$ from $x$ is of the form:

$\hat{y}=a+bx$,

where $a$ is the $y$-intercept and $b$ is the slope.

##Finding the slope

We can determine the slope as follows:

$b=r\left(\dfrac{s_y}{s_x}\right)$

In our case,

$b=0.81\left(\dfrac{177.6}{14.8}\right)=9.72$

##Finding the $y$-intercept

Because the regression line passes through the point $(\bar x, \bar y)$, we can find the $y$-intercept as follows:

$a=\bar y-b\bar x$

In our case,

$a=696 -9.72 (75.8)=-40.776$

assessment_items.question_content:
A stonemason wants to look at the relationship between the density of stones she cuts and the depth to which her abrasive water jet cuts them. The data show a linear pattern with the summary statistics shown below:

 | mean | standard deviation
- | - | -
$x=$ stone density $\left( \dfrac{\text{g}}{\text{cm}^3} \right)$  | $\bar{x}=2.5$ | $s_x=0.3$
$y=$ cutting depth $(\text{mm})$ | $\bar{y}=41.7$ | $s_y=42$
 |  | $r=-0.95$

**Find the equation of the least-squares regression line for predicting the cutting depth from the density of the stone.**  
*Round your entries to the nearest hundredth.*

$\hat y=$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$+$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$x$
hints:
##Least-squares regression equation

The equation for the least-squares regression line for predicting $y$ from $x$ is of the form:

$\hat{y}=a+bx$,

where $a$ is the $y$-intercept and $b$ is the slope.

##Finding the slope

We can determine the slope as follows:

$b=r\left(\dfrac{s_y}{s_x}\right)$

In our case,

$b=-0.95\left(\dfrac{42}{0.3}\right)=-133$

##Finding the $y$-intercept

Because the regression line passes through the point $(\bar x, \bar y)$, we can find the $y$-intercept as follows:

$a=\bar y-b\bar x$

In our case,

$a=41.7 +133 (2.5)=374.2$

##Answer

$\hat y=374.2 -133 x$

assessment_items.question_content:
A limnologist takes samples from a creek on several days and counts the numbers of flatworms in each sample. The limnologist wants to look at the relationship between the temperature of the creek and the number of flatworms in the sample. The data show a linear pattern with the summary statistics shown below:

 | mean | standard deviation
- | - | -
$x=$ creek temperature $(^\circ\text{C})$  | $\bar{x}=10.2$ | $s_x=2.8$
$y=$ number of flatworms | $\bar{y}=37.6$ | $s_y=30.8$
 |  | $r=-0.98$

**Find the equation of the least-squares regression line for predicting the number of flatworms from the creek temperature.**  
*Round your entries to the nearest hundredth.*

$\hat y=$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$+$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]$x$
hints:
##Least-squares regression equation

The equation for the least-squares regression line for predicting $y$ from $x$ is of the form:

$\hat{y}=a+bx$,

where $a$ is the $y$-intercept and $b$ is the slope.

##Finding the slope

We can determine the slope as follows:

$b=r\left(\dfrac{s_y}{s_x}\right)$

In our case,

$b=-0.98\left(\dfrac{30.8}{2.8}\right)=-10.78$

##Finding the $y$-intercept

Because the regression line passes through the point $(\bar x, \bar y)$, we can find the $y$-intercept as follows:

$a=\bar y-b\bar x$

In our case,

$a=37.6 +10.78 (10.2)=147.556$
